page_content:
PHIL269 Speical Topics in Study Abroad II (1-6 Credits) Special topics course taken as part of an approved study abroad program. Repeatable to: 15 credits if content differs.

PHIL228 Issues in History of Philosophy (3 Credits) An examination of selected issues in the history of philosophy. Repeatable to: 12 credits if content differs. Additional Information: Counts toward the history of philosophy requirement for the Philosophy major.

PHIL234 Fundamental Concepts of Judaism (3 Credits) A conceptional introduction to Judaism, analyzing its fundamental concepts from both analytical and historical perspectives. Discussion of "normative" Judaism as well as other conceptions of Judaism. Topics include: God, the Jewish people, authority, ethics, the sacred and the profane, particularism and universalism. Cross-listed with: JWST250, RELS250. Credit Only Granted for: JWST250, PHIL234, or RELS250.
